objective: Replay a synthetic chained district SSO assessment that moves from permissive SSO callback handling into overbroad roster export, vendor sync scope expansion, and weak audit coverage.
assumptions: The lab uses synthetic evidence and fake student records only.
No real identity provider, roster system, or vendor integration is contacted.
The service is intentionally vulnerable and must never be deployed.

phases.objective: Map SSO callback policy, admin session behavior, and sensitive downstream workflows.
actions: Exchange a localhost redirect into an administrator session
Identify roster export and vendor sync endpoints reachable from that session
successCriteria: SSO policy, session exchange, roster export, and vendor sync behavior are recorded as evidence
noSubagents: tenant-risk prioritization stays with the primary operator

phases.objective: Validate the chained impact across identity, data export, vendor sync, and audit surfaces.
actions: Validate administrator session creation without step-up controls
Use the session to export sensitive cross-district roster fields
Use the session to approve excessive vendor sync scopes
Inspect audit events for elevated export or vendor alerts
successCriteria: Three report-ready findings cite distinct but connected evidence sets
noSubagents: final chain severity and remediation sequencing stay with the primary operator

phases.objective: Produce a dense multi-finding report that explains the chain rather than listing isolated bugs.
actions: Run report_lint with outline section requirements
Run report_render with authored report preservation
successCriteria: Final handoff lint and operation audit pass with three reportable findings and authored-report coverage

evidence.title: Synthetic SSO callback configuration
evidence.summary: The SSO configuration allows localhost callback targets and does not require signed RelayState.

evidence.title: Synthetic SSO exchange creates admin session without step-up
evidence.summary: A localhost redirect exchange returns a district-admin session without MFA or step-up authentication.

evidence.title: Synthetic cross-district roster export
evidence.summary: The district-admin session exports sensitive fields across all district tenants.

evidence.title: Synthetic vendor sync accepts excessive scopes
evidence.summary: The district-admin session approves vendor sync access to roster, guardian, and student-support data without additional approval.

evidence.title: Synthetic audit events lack elevated chain alerts
evidence.summary: The login, export, and vendor sync chain is logged as informational activity without elevated export or vendor alerts.

findings.title: SSO callback policy enables administrator session creation without step-up
findings.description: The synthetic SSO configuration allows a localhost callback and unsigned RelayState, and the exchange returns a district-admin session without requiring MFA or a step-up challenge.
findings.impact: A stolen assertion or misdirected callback could produce an administrator session that can reach sensitive roster and vendor workflows.
findings.remediation: Restrict callback URLs to production hosts, require signed RelayState, enforce phishing-resistant administrator MFA, and require step-up authentication before sensitive workflows.

findings.title: Administrator session can export sensitive roster fields across all districts
findings.description: The synthetic roster export endpoint accepts the SSO-created administrator session and returns sensitive fields across all district tenants.
findings.impact: A compromised administrator session could expose student support status, guardian contact details, and other protected roster fields across tenant boundaries.
findings.remediation: Enforce tenant scoping server-side, restrict sensitive field export by explicit purpose, require step-up approval for bulk export, and limit export size and audience.

findings.title: Vendor sync grants excessive scopes without elevated audit coverage
findings.description: The synthetic vendor sync endpoint accepts broad roster, guardian, and student-support scopes from the administrator session while audit records remain informational.
findings.impact: A malicious or compromised administrator workflow could push sensitive student and guardian data to a vendor integration without a meaningful detection signal.
findings.remediation: Require approval for sensitive vendor scopes, split sync grants by purpose, apply least privilege to vendor tokens, and elevate audit events for export-to-sync chains.

reportIncludes: Authored SSO Roster Export Chain Assessment
The highest-priority risk is not any single endpoint by itself
pdfIncludes: Authored SSO Roster Export Chain Assessment
